Description: Osmundea hybrida genome assembly, chromosome: 9
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Rhodophyta; Florideophyceae; Rhodymeniophycidae; Ceramiales; Rhodomelaceae; Laurencieae; Osmundea
Assembly: The assembly of Osmundea hybrida (urOsmHybr2) is based on 58X PacBio data and 69X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 567 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 67.158 Mb (with the largest being 4.832 Mb). Additionally, 3722 regions totaling 741.053 Mb (with the largest being 5.167 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 139 haplotypic regions, totaling 71.3Mb, (with the largest being 2.23Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Tortricoidea; Tortricidae; Olethreutinae; Grapholitini; Pammene
Description: Pammene argyrana genome assembly, chromosome: 20
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Tortricoidea; Tortricidae; Olethreutinae; Grapholitini; Pammene
Assembly: The assembly ilPamArgy1.hap2.1 is based on 71x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes.Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 6 (0-5.3 Mbp). The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 20 (14.8 - 15.3 Mbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 24 (4.5 - 5.2 Mbp) are unknown.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Mammalia; Eutheria; Euarchontoglires; Glires; Rodentia; Hystricomorpha; Bathyergidae; Heterocephalus
Assembly: Primary assembly. paternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the paternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres.
